content.text: Now let me send the PROPOSE message to agent2.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "PROPOSE" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "" --note "My task: add ssn type to outlines/types/__init__.py after the isbn definition. This is a single-file, small addition (a new Regex-based type constant). If your feature also touches this file, propose how we split - e.g. I add my block after isbn, you add yours elsewhere in the same file, and we each only touch our own new lines to avoid clobbering."

coop-send agent2 --type "REVISE" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "We both need outlines/types/__init__.py (additive, non-overlapping blocks) so a fully disjoint file split isn't possible here. Proposal to avoid clobbering: I'll insert my ssn Regex block immediately after the existing isbn block (lines ~68-70) and save first, then send DONE. Please wait for my DONE before editing, then append your hash_sha256 block at the end of the file (or another location far from the isbn/ssn block). That way our edits touch disjoint line ranges and won't conflict. Reply ACCEPT if that works."

content.text: Agreement reached: I'll add the `ssn` block right after `isbn`, and agent2 will append their `hash_sha256` block at the end of the file. Let me send my own ACCEPT to close the handshake, then implement.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "ACCEPT" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Confirmed. I'll add ssn right after isbn and send DONE when saved; you append hash_sha256 at end of file after that."

file.content:
# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

input.new_string:
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
# Excludes area numbers 000, 666 and 900-999, and all-zero group/serial
# numbers, per SSA rules: https://www.ssa.gov/employer/randomization.html
ssn = Regex(
    r"(?!000|666|9[0-9]{2})[0-9]{3}-(?!00)[0-9]{2}-(?!0{4})[0-9]{4}"
)

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: Import works. Let's quickly test the regex pattern matches valid/invalid SSNs as expected.

input.command:
python -c "
import re
p = re.compile(r'^(?!000|666|9[0-9]{2})[0-9]{3}-(?!00)[0-9]{2}-(?!0{4})[0-9]{4}\$')
tests = {
  '123-45-6789': True,
  '000-45-6789': False,
  '666-45-6789': False,
  '900-45-6789': False,
  '123-00-6789': False,
  '123-45-0000': False,
  '123456789': False,
}
for t, expected in tests.items():
    result = bool(p.match(t))
    print(t, result, 'OK' if result==expected else 'FAIL')
"

content.text: Regex behaves correctly. Now let's check for any existing tests file for types to see the expected test pattern (though I won't add tests unless needed), and confirm the diff.

+)

All existing tests pass. Implementation is complete.

**Summary:** Added `outlines/types/__init__.py:73-77` — a new `ssn` type (`Regex`) validating US Social Security Numbers in `XXX-XX-XXXX` format, excluding invalid area numbers (000, 666, 900-999) and all-zero group/serial segments per SSA rules. Placed directly after the existing `isbn` definition as requested. Verified via import and regex matching tests, and confirmed the existing test suite still passes. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Coordinated with agent2 via the handshake protocol — we agreed to share `outlines/types/__init__.py` with disjoint insertion points (mine after `isbn`, theirs appended at file end for `hash_sha256`) to avoid clobbering.
